title: Data Ownership, Integrity, Migrations, and Lifecycle Governance · Evidence Triage
summary: A preserved decision record for data ownership, integrity, migrations, and lifecycle governance contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in governed data model and migration rehearsal and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Assign authoritative source, steward, identity, update rights, derived copies, synchronization, and dispute resolution for each entity.
Protect uniqueness, relationships, state invariants, transaction boundaries, and concurrency behavior at complementary layers.
Plan expand-migrate-contract changes with old/new reader and writer compatibility, backfill, verification, pause, and rollback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Data Owner Source
steps.instruction:
debug case — Three stores claim authority for the same entity while copied data, conflicting meanings, irreversible migration, and deletion gaps accumulate. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Assign authoritative source, steward, identity, update rights, derived copies, synchronization, and dispute resolution for each entity.

Inspect the Data Owner Source trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Data Owner Source.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for assign authoritative source, steward, identity, update rights, derived copies, synchronization, and dispute resolution for each entity. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Data Owner Source, keep Data Owner Source connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Data Owner Source, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Data Owner Source, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Data Owner Source precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Data Owner Source, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Three stores claim authority for the same entity while copied data, conflicting meanings, irreversible migration, and deletion gaps accumulate. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Assign authoritative source, steward, identity, update rights, derived copies, synchronization, and dispute resolution for each entity.
For Inspect the failure record · Debug Data Owner Source, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: The database containing a value owns its meaning.

stimulus.title: Data Owner Source case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Three stores claim authority for the same entity while copied data, conflicting meanings, irreversible migration, and deletion gaps accumulate. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Assign authoritative source, steward, identity, update rights, derived copies, synchronization, and dispute resolution for each entity.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: The database containing a value owns its meaning.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign authoritative source, steward, identity, update rights, derived copies, synchronization, and dispute resolution for each entity. Artifact under review: governed data model and migration rehearsal.

options.text: For inspect the failure record, apply Data Owner Source without its stated preconditions, omit the governed data model and migration rehearsal trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Data Owner Source for governed data model and migration rehearsal.
The record names the assumption, applies Data Owner Source, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Data Owner Source record says governed data model and migration rehearsal looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Data Owner Source.

  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Data Reconciliation structural check is only one gate. governed data model and migration rehearsal must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Data Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Data Reconciliation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Three stores claim authority for the same entity while copied data, conflicting meanings, irreversible migration, and deletion gaps accumulate. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. For Publish the incident record · Debug Data Reconciliation, keep Data Reconciliation connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reconcile counts, checksums, invariants, samples, rejects, and actor-visible outcomes before and after migration or repair.
